articles.headline: New Raptor: Why America's Top Fighter Is Trading Pure Stealth For Passive Detection & Range
articles.blurb: The US Air Force is upgrading the F-22 Raptor into a 'Raptor 2.0' configuration, adding stealthy external fuel tanks, IRST pods, and avionics upgrades...
articles.takeaway: F-22 modernization prioritizes extended range and passive sensing over traditional stealth, signaling a strategic shift toward contested airspace detection capabilities.

articles.headline: Hawaiian Airlines To Permanently Remove Airbus A321neo Flights Between Honolulu & Oakland
articles.blurb: The move will happen this October, boosting capacity by nearly 50%.
articles.takeaway: Hawaiian Airlines is strategically replacing smaller A321neo aircraft with larger widebody jets on this route to capitalize on premium long-haul demand and improve unit economics on the Honolulu-Oakland corridor.

articles.headline: Not Buying It: Southwest Airlines Criticized After Giving High-Tier Flier A Handwritten Note

articles.headline: Why Delta Air Lines Now Only Has 2 Boeing 757 Routes To Europe
articles.blurb: The carrier used to deploy the type on transatlantic duties a lot more widely and regularly.
articles.takeaway: Delta's strategic withdrawal from 757 transatlantic service reflects the aircraft's age and rising operating costs making it uncompetitive against newer widebody fuel-efficient alternatives on long-haul routes.

articles.headline: United Airlines Slashes Boeing 737 MAX 8 International Flights By 16%
articles.blurb: The type will, however, see increased usage on European routes.
articles.takeaway: United's strategic reallocation shifts 737 MAX 8 capacity from long-haul international markets toward shorter European routes, likely optimizing aircraft utilization while managing fleet diversity across regional segments.

articles.headline: SMS Declaration of Compliance
articles.blurb: What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.
articles.takeaway: Operators must formally document their safety management system procedures to demonstrate regulatory compliance and reduce enforcement risk.

articles.headline: The 7 Largest US Air Force Bases By Land Area

articles.headline: US Air Force Confronts Range Disadvantage For F-22 & F-35 Missiles Against China's PL-15
articles.blurb: US fighters face a growing missile range gap as China’s PL-15 outpaces AMRAAM, driving urgent development of next-gen weapons like AIM-260.
articles.takeaway: The PL-15's range superiority forces US fighter doctrine to shift from beyond-visual-range engagement tactics, accelerating development timelines for next-generation air-to-air missiles to restore tactical parity.

articles.headline: The New Normal? Spanish Airline Imposes Fuel Surcharge On Already-Paid Tickets
articles.blurb: This move comes as a surprise to many.
articles.takeaway: Post-purchase fuel surcharges set a risky precedent that could trigger regulatory backlash and customer defection if other carriers attempt similar tactics on locked-in fares.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS shifts aviation from responding to accidents to systematically identifying and mitigating risks before incidents occur, fundamentally changing how operators manage safety.

articles.headline: New Anti-Jam Antenna Seen on USAF F-15E Strike Eagle During Epic Fury

articles.takeaway: The DIGAR system's CRPA antenna enhances F-15E operational resilience by providing GPS anti-jamming capability, critical for contested electromagnetic environments where adversaries actively deny positioning signals.

articles.headline: Press Release: Virgin Atlantic accelerates Starlink rollout

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Integrated optimization across airline, airport, and flight operations—rather than isolated improvements—holds the key to accommodating projected air traffic growth while meeting environmental targets.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Falcon 9 launches Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the ISS

articles.headline: How Ryanair Enforces Cabin Baggage Rules
